notes: Named contact: Rebecca Jeschke. Backup: info@eff.org, Signal +1-510-243-8020. High mission alignment with ELITE threat model.
Backup: info@cdt.org. Target: Privacy & Data team or Security & Surveillance team specifically.
Also: security@accessnow.org (Digital Security Helpline). Global reach; strong focus on at-risk populations including undocumented people.
London-based. Runs active data broker campaigns. Primary-source structure of threat model is highly relevant to their methodology.
Secure contact: tor.project@protonmail.com. Corpus recommends Tor — informing them of specific use-case threat model is directly relevant.
Backup: policy@mozilla.com. Has published surveillance threat analyses; institutional amplification potential.
Action-campaign focused. Best approached after larger orgs have validated; can amplify once credibility established.
Coalition partner potential — connects digital rights with labor and immigration advocacy. Dual-track amplification.
Named contact: Albert Fox Cahn (Executive Director). NYC/state-level ICE surveillance focus — directly relevant to ELITE New York deployment.
Named contact: Jeff Chester (Executive Director). Tracks commercial data broker misuse specifically — corpus directly addresses this.
FOIA-intensive organization. Corpus cites FOIA-sourced contracts; natural credibility fit. May publish analysis or resource listing.
Grassroots; relevant to warrantless data broker purchase angle. Lower institutional amplification but activist network reach.
Director: Lorrie Faith Cranor. 50+ faculty, 100+ graduate students. Usable privacy research lineage — corpus fits research citation and teaching use cases.
Executive Director: Ann Cleaveland. Backup: shanti@berkeley.edu (philanthropy). Funds applied security research with policy implications.
Internet Policy Research Initiative. Also: CSAIL Security Seminar. Theoretical + applied crypto with policy reach.
Human-centered security research; active applied program. Allen School contact form backup.
Note: Stanford Internet Observatory ended 2024. Cyber Policy Center continues. Policy focus means faster conversion timeline than pure research programs.
Student group RPISEC engaged with community outreach — may accelerate initial response compared to pure faculty contact.
Named contact: Christopher Bavitz (Managing Director, Cyberlaw Clinic). Clinic has active caseloads — faster conversion than research faculty. Immigration law intersection strong.
Institute for Information Security & Privacy. Strong applied security program; not in original list but one of strongest US applied security programs.
Cybersecurity & Privacy Institute. Applied crypto and privacy-preserving systems — corpus methodology aligns with their research standards.
CFP: cfp@defcon.org. DEF CON 34 CFP opens late 2025. Forum: forum.defcon.org. Peer-to-peer framing required — treat as reviewer, not audience.
Annual Congress in Hamburg. Submission portal: events.ccc.de. Strong civil liberties tradition; ELITE threat model directly fits CCC interest areas.
CFP opens annually ~January. Corporate/government audience; reaches CISO community who should understand ELITE data purchases.
Washington DC–based. Accepts community talk proposals. Good venue for source protection and opsec content — smaller scale but influential.
Approach via published contact info (Mastodon, security.txt, conference networking). Target 3–5 researchers who publish on surveillance tech or law enforcement data pipelines.
Also: press@freedom.press. Builds SecureDrop; corpus recommends it. Natural partner. High probability of republication or resource citation.
NICAR conference covers digital security tools. Missouri School of Journalism base. Training channel is primary target.
International focus but digital security content translates globally. Digital safety team is the right routing target.

org_name: Reporters Committee for Freedom of the Press (RCFP)
notes: Legal Hotline: 800-336-4243. Best approach: frame corpus as source protection resource for journalists covering immigration enforcement.
SPJ Journalists Toolbox covers digital security. Corpus could be integrated as a resource link — low-friction adoption path.
Relevant given specific at-risk population covered in threat model. Dual relevance: journalist source protection + community members covered by ELITE.
AAPI communities disproportionately surveilled. Journalist source protection + community threat model both apply.
